heading: Special pay: diving duty
label: 304 Special pay: diving duty
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)304(a)304(b)37:236(a), (b).37:236(c)., ; –(10), ; , , .Oct. 12, 1949, ch. 681, § 20563 Stat. 810Mar. 31, 1955, ch. 20, § 2(8)69 Stat. 21Pub. L. 87–145, § 175 Stat. 382Aug. 17, 1961In subsection (a), the last sentence is substituted for section 236(b) of existing title 37. The word “competent” is omitted as surplusage.

Amendments1999—Subsec. (b). , substituted “$240” for “$200” and “$340” for “$300”.Pub. L. 106–65, § 617(a)Subsec. (c). , amended subsec. (c) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (c) read as follows: “A member may be paid special pay under this section and incentive pay under for the same period of service only if the member is assigned by orders to a hazardous duty described in in addition to diving duty. However, if a member is paid special pay under this section, the member is not entitled to more than one payment of incentive pay under .”Pub. L. 106–65, § 617(b)section 301 of this titlesection 301(a) of this titlesection 301 of this title1998—Subsec. (a)(3). amended par. (3) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (3) read as follows: “actually performs diving duty.”Pub. L. 105–2611991—Subsec. (a). struck out “of this section” after “subsection (b)”.Pub. L. 102–25Subsec. (b). struck out “of this section” after “subsection (a)”.Pub. L. 102–251987—Subsecs. (d), (e). added subsec. (d) and redesignated former subsec. (d) as (e).Pub. L. 100–1801981— revised provisions of subsec. (a) into new subsecs. (a), (b), and (c), redesignated subsec. (b) as (d), and, in provisions of subsec. (a) as revised, added to enumeration of conditions attached to entitlement to special pay requirement that the member maintain proficiency as a diver by frequent and regular dives, substituted a rate of $200 a month for officers and $300 a month for enlisted men for former rate of $110 a month for all members, and inserted provisions authorizing payment of both special pay under this section and incentive pay under in specified circumstances.Pub. L. 97–60section 301 of this title

A, title VI, § 617(c)113 Stat. 652Oct. 5, 1999“The amendments made by subsections (a) and (b) [amending this section] shall take effect on , and shall apply with respect to special pay paid under such section for months beginning on or after that date.”October 1, 1999

Effective Date of 1998 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–261, div. A, title VI, § 616(b)112 Stat. 2041Oct. 17, 1998“The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall take effect on , and shall apply with respect to months beginning on or after that date.”October 1, 1998

Effective Date of 1987 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–180, div. A, title VI, § 624(b)101 Stat. 1104Dec. 4, 1987“The amendments made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall take effect on the first day of the fourth calendar month following the month in which this Act is enacted [Dec. 1987] and shall apply only with respect to diving duty performed on or after that date.”

children.text: is assigned by orders to the duty of diving;
is required to maintain proficiency as a diver by frequent and regular dives; and

children.label: A
children.text: actually performs diving duty while serving in an assignment for which diving is a primary duty; or

children.label: B
children.text: meets the requirements to maintain proficiency as described in paragraph (2) while serving in an assignment that includes diving duty other than as a primary duty.

children.label: b
children.text: Special pay payable under subsection (a) shall be paid at a rate of not more than $240 a month, in the case of an officer, and at a rate of not more than $340 a month, in the case of an enlisted member.

children.label: c
children.text: If, in addition to diving duty, a member is assigned by orders to one or more hazardous duties described in , the member may be paid, for the same period of service, special pay under this section and incentive pay under such section 301 for each hazardous duty for which the member is qualified.section 301 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: for each regular period of instruction, or period of appropriate duty, at which the member is engaged for at least two hours, including that performed on a Sunday or holiday; or

children.label: B
children.text: for the performance of such other equivalent training, instruction, duty, or appropriate duties, as the Secretary may prescribe under .section 206(a) of this title
This subsection does not apply to a member who is entitled to basic pay under .section 204 of this title

children.label: e
children.text: In time of war, the President may suspend the payment of diving duty pay.
